name: UK Architectural Exterior Photography - Intermediate Guide

step.name: Pick the shot type before you arrive
step.text: Decide the day before. One of four shot types — heritage façade, modern glass, urban context, abstract detail — with a specific target building. Drives lens, time of day, conditions, parking. Pick the headline frame, commit, build session around delivering it.

step.name: Read the forecast and match conditions to subject
step.text: Check Met Office 24-48 hours out. Modern glass loves dramatic cloud. Heritage stone reads better in clean blue. Brutalism wants midday sun. Switch shot type if forecast doesn't match. Check sunrise/sunset times. Check tide if coastal/riverside.

step.name: Arrive early and walk before you set up
step.text: 45 minutes before golden moment for blue/sunset; 20 minutes for daylight. Walk the building. Full circle if accessible. Ground-level, mid-level, elevated viewpoints. Use PhotoPills or Sun Surveyor. Mental-map 3-4 target compositions before raising camera.

step.name: Set up deliberately and dial in settings
step.text: Tripod at chosen viewpoint height. Camera back parallel to building if correcting verticals; tilted if embracing lean. Aperture priority f/8-f/11, ISO 100, RAW. Wide-margin capture for Lightroom correction. Single-point AF on distant detail. Expose for sky in blue hour.

step.name: Work the moment, then move
step.text: 3-5 exposures at bracketed shutter speeds per composition. Wait for interruptions to clear. Watch for exterior lights coming on at dusk — strongest 5-minute window of heritage-stone sessions. 20-minute hard deadline per location. Move to next composition while light still good.

step.name: Cull, correct perspective, edit lightly
step.text: Cull on import — sharp frames with workable verticals. 4-10 keepers from 60-90 minute session. Lightroom Transform Auto first, then Guided. Crop after correction. Edit lightly: tonal balance, restrained contrast, careful colour. Heavy correction needed = capture wasn't strong enough.
